full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Broad screening of papers matching CORE A* venues and 2009-2012 constraints while avoiding duplicates from the class response sheet.
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: I have to select a research paper which fulfils some constraints which are mentioned in the midsem_PartA pdf which I have provided to you, list down the requirements and find a research paper which fulfils all the criteria mentioned in the pdf and also remember that don't take the papers which are already taken by the other students I have uploaded the excel sheet of the already taken research papers
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Identified the initially proposed 'Discourse Parser' paper and confirmed it was not present in the current submission list.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I checked the official CORE portal to confirm the venue ranking and searched the live response sheet for the suggested paper title.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Feasibility assessment of dataset requirements and searching for alternative papers to avoid licensed LDC data risks.
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: does this fulfils the dataset criteria... and if yes then find some dataset for it and also tell find 2 more papers may be different and their dataset for these for me to choose from 3 papers that what should I prefer
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Flagged potential licensing issues with the first paper and suggested 2 more papers - Hierarchical SVM paper and Multi-class L2,1-norm SVM with bio datasets from which I chose the ICML 2011 Hierarchical SVM paper as a safer alternative with public data.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I researched the 'GUM' corpus and LIBSVM archives to verify that the suggested datasets were truly open-access.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Detailed constraint validation for the 'Hierarchical Classification via Orthogonal Transfer' paper.
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: tell me how is this research paper "Hierarchical SVM with orthogonality regularizer" can I choose it? analyze that this research paper follows the constraints or not and if yes then provide all the information about it like official link, dataset link
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Confirmed the paper is from 2011, ICML (CORE A*), and uses public RCV1 text data, making it a highly compliant choice.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I cross-referenced the publication year (2011) and venue (ICML) with the CORE ranking guidelines provided in the exam PDF.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Verifying the methodological nature of the contribution to ensure it is not just a benchmark or dataset paper.
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: does this paper's contribution follows this The primary contribution must be methodological or an application of the above algorithms. Papers whose main contribution is only a dataset, benchmark, or system are NOT allowed.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Explained the novel orthogonality regularizer and RDA optimization innovation, confirming it is a methodological contribution.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I skimmed the technical report's math section to confirm the presence of the custom objective function described by the LLM.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Assessing reproducibility and implementation difficulty for a standard CPU-only laptop.
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: can this paper be reproducible or implemented using coding and if yes is it easy and if yes then can you find some repository which is available
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Confirmed the algorithm is CPU-friendly and identified that while no official 2011 repo exists, the technical report provides sufficient pseudocode.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I downloaded the technical report (MSR-TR-2011-54) and verified that Algorithm 1 provides the steps needed for the RDA variant implementation.

top_5_prompts.prompt: I have to select a research paper which fulfils some constraints... find a research paper... and don't take the papers which are already taken by the other students
top_5_prompts.why_important: This was the primary prompt for identifying a unique paper that adhered to the specific venue and year constraints of the exam.

top_5_prompts.prompt: tell me how is this research paper "Hierarchical SVM with orthogonality regularizer" can I choose it? analyze that this research paper follows the constraints...
top_5_prompts.why_important: This prompt provided the final validation for the selected paper, ensuring it hit all the mandatory requirements for method alignment and venue.

top_5_prompts.prompt: can this paper be reproducible or implemented using coding and if yes is it easy and if yes then can you find some repository which is available
top_5_prompts.why_important: Crucial for assessing feasibility on a personal laptop, a key requirement for the later reproduction phase in Part B.

top_5_prompts.prompt: does this paper's contribution follows this The primary contribution must be methodological or an application of the above algorithms...
top_5_prompts.why_important: Ensured that the paper's core value was mathematical/methodological rather than just a dataset, which is a grounds for disqualification.

top_5_prompts.prompt: does this fulfils the dataset criteria... find 2 more papers may be different and their dataset for these for me to choose from.
top_5_prompts.why_important: Helped identify the risk of restricted-access datasets in the initially proposed paper, leading to a safer, more compliant selection.

student_declaration.statement: I declare that this JSON file contains a complete and honest record of my LLM usage for Part A.
